_rationale:
NOTHING is hidden from the scan -- every element, including chart internals, is evaluated. levelCapWhenNeedsReview caps axe results flagged needs-review (axe's `incomplete` bucket: 'axe could not determine this; a human must verify it') to WARNING instead of ERROR. Genuine axe `violations` are untouched and still fail as errors (see node_modules/pa11y/lib/runners/axe.js: only the incomplete set is passed needsFurtherReview=true). This surfaces -- rather than hides -- axe's uncertainty. The only items this affects here are axe's `bgOverlap` color-contrast incompletes (contrastRatio reported as 0 because axe's pixel sampler cannot resolve a background near the JS-rendered Highcharts SVG/foreignObject labels): the decorative chart-internal text, and three real HTML elements directly beneath the full-width inaction bar chart (the data-table , the methodology

, and its DOI link). Those three were MANUALLY reviewed and computed at 10.37:1, 9.64:1, and 8.41:1 -- far above the 4.5:1 AA threshold (see docs/ACCESSIBILITY.md for the measurement). Charts are progressive enhancement: the accessible representation is the always-present data ; a static graphic renders with scripting off. Note: chromeLaunchConfig (executablePath / --no-sandbox) is environment-specific and supplied at runtime, e.g. --config of a local file that merges this one.
